probe_method: Test three failure modes: unknown model name, malformed JSON payload, invalid model parameters (num_ctx above max). Verify distinguishable errors.

probes.error_body: {"error":"invalid character '{' looking for beginning of object key string"}

probes.response: Normal response returned — Ollama silently accepted invalid num_ctx
probes.note: Ollama does not validate num_ctx bounds. Silently accepts absurd values without error or warning. Response was normal (8 eval_count tokens). Likely clamped internally but no signal to client.

evidence_summary: 2/3 failure modes produce clear, distinguishable HTTP errors (404 for unknown model, 400 for malformed JSON). Invalid model parameters (num_ctx=999999999) are silently accepted with HTTP 200 — no validation error. Partial: Ollama reliably reports structural errors but does not validate parameter bounds.
workaround: Dispatcher should validate num_ctx client-side before sending to Ollama. Do not rely on Ollama for parameter validation.
